label: Complaint
comment:
The assignments presented an overwhelming workload. Whilst the content of the assignments, and concepts were great, simply giving us a 'reference implementation' and instructing us to copy its behaviour felt not only lazy, but the majority of the assignment time was not practical, and felt like a chore. This includes the tedious ordering of the error cases we had to reverse-engineer for every task, alongside the very strange edge cases which took a very long time to test. The course itself was great, but the assignments were extremely sub-par in terms of enjoyment, and were enough to bring the overall satisfaction of the course down. It felt like those in charge of the assignments were bullying or hazing students. Not to mention the extremely passive-aggressive, non-informative forum responses from certain tutors.

This brings me onto my complaints with X, the course admin for the term. I would like to preface this by stating that I bear no personal grudge to X , or any course staff. This is my viewpoint from my experience throughout the term.

The first in a long list of issues is that the manner in which he carried himself throughout the term for students was extremely disingenuous - the level of disrespect that students faced when interacting with X , whether it be online or offline, has no place in an educational setting. As ridiculous as this may sound, it felt as if he enjoyed mistreating students, not helping them, and making them trudge through tasks with no direction throughout the term. I say this because of his incredible lack of detail and careless language when answering forum posts, which felt deliberate. As an educator myself, I understand having frustrations with students, especially those who choose not to listen, however this is no manner in which to treat them. I have had friends and colleagues who have interacted with X in the past, whether it be in course discussions, or tutorials, and all tend to agree. In the most professional sense, he does not offer, nor encourage a positive educational experience. As a student, I felt put down despite coming here to learn, as dealing with X felt like a punishment within itself. 

The course staff overall were amazing. X  is a fantastic lecturer - informative, encouraging of new things, witty, and approachable. He was blunt, so to speak, in a good way. I felt like X , alongside my tutors and other staff I have interacted with throughout the term, have encouraged me to learn in a positive way. I would very highly reconsider having X  as staff in future.

Thank you to the rest of the team.

label: Complaint
comment:
First and foremost, i do not blame X for the organisation of the course, she worked extremely hard to allow for this to run as smoothly as possible, and it was evident that she has worked hard to make this an enjoyable final studio. However, when there is no hand over from previous course convenors, this makes it an extremely disorganised studio.  For future studios, please put the effort into providing a proper handover, its horrific that a well established university relies on hard workers, like X , to try and figure out everything on the fly, and ultimately reflects poorly on the university, not the convenor. 

I have never done a design studio where the brief has constantly changed. For regional studio in particular when you are only at site for a select amount of time, it is crucial that every detail is confirmed and remains unchanged, because in all honestly i didn't even know how to progress with my design because there was constantly factors that were changing. I think this is the worst studio i've done during my bachelor degree, and i'm sure im not the only person who thinks this. 

Also i think some feedback should be provided to all tutors to not laugh at peoples designs, even outside of this studio. That is not CONSTRUCTIVE feedback, its just belittling. Maybe run a training course for anyone who is going to be a future tutor on what constructive criticism actually is as we are STUDENTS, we are learning, the point of learning is to make mistakes and learn from it, but when we are put in an environment where our work is laughed at, it's extremely discouraging. In my opinion this is the design studio that made me decide architecture is not for me 

I hope X gets a well deserved break at the end of this term!!

label: Complaint
comment:
I usually refrain from writing  comments since these surveys aren't even anonymous so we're essentially forced to tread super carefully and not say anything negative but this course has hit a new low for UNSW standards. 

The whole course was put together in a very lazy way. The lecture slides are way too short, unhelpful in answering questions, and just plain useless. While the tutorial questions are nice, the provided solutions are essentially useless, skipping so many steps that they are often incomprehensible. The lecturers did not provide enough resources to help with the assignments nor enough resources to help practice for the exam, with almost no practice questions and zero past papers available.

Additionally, the teachers were late in responding to questions posted on the forum. The course was not engaging at all.

label: Complaint
comment:
Having been taught these chemistry concepts in previous courses, I am able to compare the layout of lectures to one another and highlight how complicated and confusing the X lectures are. I think that the lectures went into way too much detail for some concepts, making things seem way more confusing than they actually were and quite overwhelming at times. I think that if the concepts were not delved into in so much detail, the whole chemical process/topic would appear much less overwhelming and be a lot easier to grasp.  

I had a bad experience with X, as he screamed at me in front of a whole class. I was treated as a 5 year old and not the adult capable of a discussion that I am. It was disappointing to be treated in this way by a staff member.

label: Complaint
keywords: Lectures did not explain content very well
comment:
Labs were far too repetitious and time consuming, recording values again and again for what? The effects could be shown without spending 3 hours a week writing down frequencies and voltages. The Pre-labs also took nearly as long and seemed disconnected from the course slightly. There was also no reason to purchase that lab manual, I like writing on paper sure but that should be preference rather than mandatory for marks - feels like a money grab especially with the editorial issues in Lab 2 and the title.

Lectures did not explain content very well, they were behind schedule and hard to follow. Giving lectures in workshop times (which weren't accessible to half the students as there are 2 wks slots) was not really understandable to me. In the end, I had to rely solely upon summary videos to get an understanding as to what I was doing.

Assignments:
Great in theory but def. not suited to a trimester. Jesus 4 assignments in 10 weeks along with weekly 3h labs and prelabs and biweekly quizzes and a final to top it off?! Maybe ass1 was tolerable even though it took forever. ass2 was easier but still took some time. practical project took absolutely forever and in hindsight I should have abandoned it before wasting so much time that's left me no time to study for other assignments. Couldn't even start the lab project.

Quizzes:
No. please no. That was a head scratcher how 20 questions in 20 minutes at the end of a lecture that required calculations was appropriate, not even able to schedule it before 12:55 so that it wouldn't interfere with the next class. Preventing those inside the lecture room from speaking about it but allowing anyone from doing it from home. The questions mostly didn't even make any sense or had incorrect values! Not even the quiz content seemed that reflective of lecture content. Only after the first few did it get better but that was only because the questions came from the same QB as the practice ones and had multiple attempts. It's better IMO to do quizzes with instant feedback, less stressful and getting to know actually whats the correct answer/why with the brief descriptions in moodle quizzes.

label: Complaint
comment:
X  had a tendency to interupt people while they were speaking.  He often came across as extremely abnoxious. He often had internet issue and dropped out at least once or twice during every lecture. 

He gave misleading guidance on the 1st case study where he told the whole class they did not have to provide a reccomendation, providing an update on one of the communication boards with a specific question. He then proceeded to mark the question with an expectation that students provided a reccomendation. This was moving the goal posts and caused a lot of people to get lower marks. In this assignment he didnt like my writing style and accused me of not addressing the specific questions. When I compared my assignment to the HD examples the same information was provided just in a different format. This was extremely dissapointing that he couldnt adapt to a variety of writing styles to analyse the responses and determine the same information was being addressed.

On another occasion he failed to correctly upload the weekly contribution questions correctly causing these to be available late with an expectation that everybody still completed it within the same timeframe. 

On one occasion he was presenting the lecture from a different country and was in his hotel room. During the session the management came in to tell him he was required to checkout and there was an extremely awkward encounter when he was rude to the people trying to get in and clean the room. This was extremely unprofessional. I would have expected him to organise his time more appropriately so our learning was not interrupted.

Overall X seemed to struggle with Moodle which I think led to some of the instances above. He also seemed to be a little bit disorganised trying to manage presenting from different locations. He should also probably consider upgrading his internet to avoid connection issues.

X should take a more flexible approach when reviewing different writing styles rather than expecting students to conform to his prefered style.

label: Complaint
comment: There were numerous resources and prescribed readings available to consult, however I wish we spent hands-on time in the Excel financial modelling and assumption decision-making. While I understand it’s not a X course, I suggest AGSM examine whether students are getting the hands-on skills throughout the DCF learning journey versus theory. It’s a very fascinating topic area but I sometimes felt technically inept. Perhaps there’s opportunity to have less breakout discussion and more in-depth run throughs of a simulated transaction? The course workload was more intensive than any other AGSM course I’ve taken to date. As a working professional I found it challenging to maximise my learning due to being time poor and so much to read/practice/study. The assessments on face-value did not fall short of what I’d expect in an M&A course, however I found it particularly challenging to write an acquisition strategy in a geography with so few potential takeover targets (e.g. caught in a loop of writing a strategy, finding minimal targets, and restarting). I would not suggest changing the assessment but perhaps broadening the scope of starting companies. Lastly, I was disappointed we did not spend time covering IPOs in more detail (understanding these are divestments).

label: Complaint
keywords: did not take the task seriously
did not have an effective way to record
comment:
The classroom arrangement is unreasonable. It's too large and empty, with everyone seated in groups far apart. It's difficult to hear what other group members are saying during class activities. I suggested to the lecturer from the first class to use a microphone, but unfortunately, it was never adopted. 
The assessment grading was not timely. By the time the first individual assignment's grades and feedback were released, the second assignment was already near its deadline. Even after we submitted the group assignment, the second assignment's grades and feedback were not out yet. The feedback for the first assignment was a bit irrelevant and not specific, making me feel the grader did not take the task seriously. 
A significant part of the course relies on class participation grades, around 25%, but the lecturer did not have an effective way to record students who contributed or spoke in class, at least not for X . 
Some lecturer did not put much effort into the course; their speaking volume during explanations was not enough for the whole class to hear clearly, and they merely read the lecture slides in a monotone voice, failing to inspire or encourage students. 
I hope some assessments with less subjective criteria can be included, at least for a portion of the grade.

label: Complaint

avoid speaking in a monotonous manner
need to put in more effort
pay more attention to students' immediate reactions
comment: X 's teaching could be improved by accepting feedback from students. During lectures, he should pay attention to his volume and the variation in his tone. As international students, English is not our first language, but we are taught to capture the audience's attention when doing presentations and to avoid speaking in a monotonous manner or just reading off slides; otherwise, we would receive low marks. You might be a great researcher, but in terms of teaching, I think you need to put in more effort and pay more attention to students' immediate reactions and feedback after class.

comment:
the syllabus might be a bit difficult considering we only have 10 weeks to study
consolidation is already a difficult part of the course, the addition of joint ventures and associates is a bit unnecessary

the structure of how the consolidation statement should be delivered is great:
i.e 
part 1 - consolidation principle
part 2 - intragroup transaction
part 3 - nci
part 4 - indirect nci

but as said, the joint ventures and associate might also need to be separated.

either that, or if it's possible to be replaced with other topics, 
honestly, there is literally too much journal entries to memorize, bear in mind these are students with no background in corporate accounting. 

also the assignment was honestly too open ended (?) 
it's just that, we thought we have fulfilled all needed requirements and that's that
we were surprised that the marking was so strict, especially that we couldn't even use what we learned in lecture into the assignment.

so since the assignment seems to be taking our time to learn about it, plus the fact that we also had to learn quite heavy topics,
i'd suggest that, either provide a heavy assignment with lenient markings
or provide a detailed assignment with strict markings

quizzes are really helpful and relevant props to that!

label: Complaint
keywords: feedback was given to us at the very last moment
comment:
1: assignments expectations across all streams were not fair, and distributed evenly.

2: feedback was given to us at the very last moment, including marks

3: our “feedback” did not reflect the marks 

4: the assignment structure kept changing over and over again, leading us to delay our progression to complete assignments especially when it came to model making

5: feedback that was given to us, did not encourage us to “do better” feedback was not constructive, it was more towards negativity and “lacked” in areas to which they (our tutors) could not help us, or suggest a clear direction to improve.

6: personally my instructors were sometimes rude, arrogant, they had no empathy or sympathy for when we needed help, they always implied us, that our issues are not a  “big deal”. 

7: assignment deliverables were inconsistent, my case, since my building was 30+ storeys high, I had to produce more work under the same time frame as those who had half or even less storeys of orthographics to complete the tasks at hand

8: not in my situation, but I have heard from our instructors giving feedback to my peers to, “look at this persons work and get inspired”, once they did get “inspired” by their fellow peers, our instructors would then say “don’t follow what others are doing”

9: 100% there was a bit of favouritism in the class, our instructors implied to us about who’s work is better than the rest

10: in my opinion I do not appreciate wasting money and paper on expensive panels to print every presentation, when these panels aren’t looked at all, when we have the presentation slides. It is simply wasteful. Either we have physical panels that we present with, or we have presentation slides to show with our models, having all of this together was double handling, excessive work load and unnecessary stress on students.

I think this course has been quite overwhelming for me,

The instructors did their best to teach each lesson which I greatly appreciate but personally I have been struggling trying to grasp all the details. Week by week I have a decent understanding of the topic and can complete the tutorial exercises but as more content was introduced each week it became a bit overwhelming as some parts overlapped. I've been reading the textbook, watching the lectures and completing the course content and I've found it a bit hard in some cases such as some little things would confuse me like the journal entries being slightly differently structured in the textbook or the act of combining journal entries which could be stressful under time constraints. 

I've complied notes of everything I could and have been dedicating many many hours each week to practice and understand which hopefully would allow me to do well on the exam. I just believe the course content could be simplified a little more as it was a big jump in skill from say X .

label: Complaint
comment:
This is honestly one of the worst subjects I have ever taken in UNSW. The entire course was disorganised and a mess. I felt like I was in the 9th circle of hell while doing this course. It is supposed to be a capstone course, why do we have so many segues? This actually made us lose our momentum and focus. And the segues are so disconnected to the actual focus of the course. For a capstone course, I am mad at how low the weighting is for the project itself, and instead putting so much weight on the reflections. Have reflections, fine, but not as 40% of the grade. And honestly, 10 reflections, then randomly grading 4 is annoying. Plus, having high expectations for the reflections but not even giving us the weekend to do it is atrocious. Honestly, the reflections made an already terrible course worse. 

We learned nothing about how to be consultants here. I was expecting more practical assessments, like ppt layout, selling ideas, how to think. Instead, we got a rubbish argument-based assessment that was so bloody disorganized and off-topic. We could have used week 6 to refine our presentations, but instead, we wasted our time on that nonsense. 

It is hypocritical how they expect so much from us, but couldn't be organised themselves. There are times Moodle is not up to date, or we have multiple submission links for the same thing, but yet, no clarity what are they for. 

Additionally, the teaching team is very inconsistent in their deadlines. Tutor will say one date, while Moodle says otherwise. Sometimes, the deadlines do not even appear in Moodle. This isn't the only subject that I am taking, at least make your deadlines and expectations clear. 

I learned absolutely nothing from this course and I'm honestly regretting my decision to sign up for this class and pay the tuition for it. The only thing that made it worthwhile is my group became close.

comment:
There is really something need to be improved. I am not only talking about this course but the whole Electrical Engineering teaching faculty.

2. Who decide the camera location in the lecture room so far away from the blackboard? Who decide thee camera location in the lecture room can only film the half of the blackboard. You are really a genius. And the faculty doesn't realize this is a problem is really another miracle. GOOD JOB!!

3. The lecture recording's quality is really bad as shit due to the reason2. I can't see anything on the board  which is impossible for students to follow the recording. This is really a big ISSUE that  drives people mad. There is so much knowledge needed to absorb in this course and this really gives people a feeling that the teaching is not really care about students' feeling and don't really want to offer much help and guidance. 

4. If this still happen in the future, then I would say you are welcome to rob me directly by asking high tuition fee but offering so less help.

comment:
Loved the course overall. Having completed 7 subjects so far Economics alongside Strategy Intensive in Term 2 2023 with X were my two favourite courses so far.

- Course material was very well structured and there was a good balance of theory & practice through exercises and discussions forum posts
- The group assignment was very interesting and relevant for me and finding out something new about an area I was quite interested in by learning with others and applying course concepts
- At time of writing this I'm still in the process of writing my final assignment but it is quite 'fun' to work on it applying course concepts by learning more about my focus country, which was actually one of 2 countries I had on my shortlist for my next holiday so great time to look into it a bit closer.
- Really enjoyed the new Intensive mode of 4 Sunday's such a great study mode giving me at least 1 day of in the Intensive weeks. If I had the choice between the standard intensives and this new mode I would prefer this.

Feedback for AGSM: Please offer more courses in this mode or F2F mode in general! I really dislike the move that almost all courses are offered online only these day with very limited F2F options! Not worth the money we are paying for at AGSM to mainly get Online Only offers.

label: Complaint
comment:
Feedback on the delivery of this class
I was most disappointed in the actual weekly classes. The online mode was by far the worst mode so far at AGSM and not worth the money I paid for. We spend approx 20% of time of the class time on course content and the rest of class time was spend on question on assignments (in the first few weeks) where it felt like people haven't read the assessment criteria or in breakout rooms and then having individuals brief back on their stories in the breakout rooms which took up a lot of class time. I would have preferred using the classes to go through the actual course material and hear from X on some real world examples (60% to 70%  of class time) then there could be 20% of class time in breakout rooms and the balance of 10% to 20% on general Q&A. 


Feedback for AGSM: 
It is very disappointing to have this subject not offered at all in F2F mode and the general trend of AGSM going towards online delivery modes and away from F2F. This was my first course undertaken online and by a large margin was the worst delivery mode and I'm being charged as much for F2F modes. Lucky ahead for me is the Executive Year which is in person. I initially planned to go with the MBAX stream but due to the direction AGSM is going away from F2F offering this is no longer an option for me. 

The M&A course would be perfect for an Intensive Mode in weeks 4 & 9. Week 4 is covering the first 4 weeks and get students ready for the individual assignment and Week 9 is covering the balance of the material and again getting students ready for the group assignment. This would have been a much better learning experience.

label: Complaint
comment:
I didn't enjoy the delivery mode of this subject at all. This is the 2nd time I had X and last time it was in the X which I rate as one of my Top 2 classes (both for content and delivery mode) alongside Economics with X in Intensive mode. However M&A in the Online mode ranks at the bottom of my list mainly driven by the learning experience the Online mode provided compared to all other F2F classes I had so far.
More time should have been spend on actually going through the course content and hear from X on her industry experiences or even bring in guest speakers as it was done in Strategy, but this delivery mode had very little of this. 

Again as mentioned before negative feedback is more so directed at AGSM by offering less and less F2F classes and moving to online classes which just have a way worse learning experience for me and are not worth the money I'm being charged for.

comment:
This is not how DSA should be taught.

Providing students with entire code and asking them to fill in the blanks in a few lines of code is not how you test DSA concepts.

Quizzes and Assignments could have used Edstem's coding platform so that students could write the ENTIRE CODE THEMSELVES and submit it with auto marking.  There are so many platforms if you guys are unable to use Edstem properly.

The lecturer has such a strong Chinese accent, WHY? Get a lecturer like X from X.

And whats with reading from slides or in this case a git repo. 

DSA is NOT A JOKE. Its very crucial when students apply for jobs and you guys have taken it very lightly. GET YOUR SHIT TOGETHER UNSW.

A better teaching plan -
1 week of C concepts (because students are doing this course after X)
Go hard on different data structures and algorithms
All sorting algorithms
LLs, graphs, maps
bitwise ops

Students pay a lot and frankly they are not getting any value in return from this course.

comment:
I had never been exposed to majority of the content and frameworks, which allowed for a lot of growth and learning. Viewing my organisation from different perspectives and challenging my current position was really interesting. 

I really enjoyed having the opportunity to discuss the case study content with my fellow students. It was great to engage in a more visual and real time setting and take discussions and dialogues out of the online setting. It would be great to see this course and more courses use a variety of dialogue settings (written and videoconferences) to assess the performance of students in the future. This would allow additional flexibility in how the courses are delivered and allow students to elect either a written or verbal assessment according to their strengths.

comment:
To Dr X, with all due respect, with all due respect, with all due respect
please, please improve your general ability as to talking, giving speeches, explaining things.
please, please, learn from Professor X, learn how to teach. 

I think giving lecture is an important part of your job as a lecturer in this class. And to be honest, you are doing it terribly, and it affects EVERY student in this class.

It's not about accent, not at all, every teacher in unsw has his/her accent. 

1. There are simply too many "background noise", useless information in your talking.
2. You skip explaining some of the important definitions, or barely, vaguely poorly explain it. 
3. Your logic is confusing; you often repeat yourself several times in a sentence without making things clear.

I find myself having to listen to the same part over and over and over again and still failing to  understand what are you talking about.
I find myself frustrated, tired, painful in the process of learning.
And I know that veryone has things they are good at and things they are not good at, which is perfectly normal. Therefore, If this is truly difficult for you, perhaps you should let another teacher handle it.

(I believe you are a wonderful reseacher in your area and I respect you as a person and absolutely have no bias or negative thoughts about you.)


About assessment:
It's poorly structured.
The lab is more about coding, which is simply not taught in the lectures. Students come from different background, some of us find it not relevant and hard to finish without a lot of help.
And group project, I don't even want to talk about group project. I almost feel like we are learning Machine learning or deep learning instead of computer vision.
I feel like the task is too big, not closely relevant, especially difficult because of the requirement of  group effort.
